definitions.text: The partly frozen, crystalline state of water that falls from the atmosphere as precipitation in flakes; also, the falling of such flakes; and the accumulation of them on the ground or on objects as a white layer. An instance of the falling of snow (etymology 1, noun sense 1); a snowfall; also, a snowstorm.
examples: We have had several heavy snows this year.

definitions.text: An instance of the falling of snow (etymology 1, noun sense 1); a snowfall; also, a snowstorm.
examples: We have had several heavy snows this year.

definitions.text: Preceded by the dummy subject it: to have snow (noun etymology 1, noun sense 1) fall from the atmosphere.
examples: It is snowing. It started to snow.

definitions.text: Of a thing: to fall like snow.
Done because the alternate outcome is very unlikely to pass.
A square-rigged sailing vessel similar to a brig formerly used as a warship, with a foremast, a mainmast, and a trysail mast immediately abaft (behind) the mainmast.
